subject: Public Health
title: Anxiety Disorders
description: A fast breakdown of Anxiety Disorders for builders.

hook.text: Anxiety is your brain's alarm system—helpful in danger, devastating when it won't turn off.

buildup.text: Generalized anxiety, panic disorder, social anxiety, and phobias affect 301 million people globally.

discovery.text: The amygdala fires constantly in anxiety disorders—perceiving threats where none exist.

twist.text: Anxiety disorders often start in childhood and go unrecognized—dismissed as shyness or nervousness.

climax.text: Cognitive behavioral therapy rewires anxious thinking patterns—and works as well as medication for many.

punchline.text: An alarm that never stops ringing isn't protection—it's a disorder.

quiz.question: How many people globally are affected by anxiety disorders?
